danger_signals.visual_form: alien creature designs with aggressive postures and hostile animations
danger_signals.meaning: environmental threat; immediate physical danger requiring evasion or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: armed alien captors with laser weapons, distinct from wildlife; military bearing and formations
danger_signals.meaning: faction-based threat; organized opposition requiring tactical avoidance or combat with armed enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: cave systems, tower structures with lethal elements; water sequences with oxygen dependency
danger_signals.meaning: world-state danger; puzzle-based obstacles that require environmental navigation to survive

danger_signals.visual_form: visual feedback from enemy laser fire and melee attacks; instantaneous game-over state
danger_signals.meaning: one-hit lethality system; any visible contact with hazard = immediate failure

danger_signals.visual_form: subterranean prison camp design with containment architecture
danger_signals.meaning: world-state: oppressive regime; captivity and confinement as narrative/environmental threat

safety_signals.visual_form: designated safe zones where player can save progress via alphanumeric code
safety_signals.meaning: narrative respawn points; world-state stability markers allowing indefinite retry

safety_signals.visual_form: friendly humanoid alien character with cooperative animation; distinct visual design from captors
safety_signals.meaning: faction ally; visual distinction from hostile aliens indicates partnership and narrative progression

safety_signals.visual_form: fallen enemy weapon pickup; visible armament change in player character
safety_signals.meaning: progression marker; transition from defenseless to combat-capable state

safety_signals.visual_form: climbing out of water, ascending tower structure, flying escape on creature
safety_signals.meaning: world-state liberation; visual trajectory from captivity toward freedom

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: humanoid alien soldier design; military bearing distinct from wildlife
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: armed soldiers with laser weapons; hierarchy implied through capture/interrogation roles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: subterranean prison camp architecture; organized military infrastructure contrasts with natural cave systems

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: non-humanoid creature designs; aggressive animalistic appearance
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: variety of creature types; some small enough to kick, others requiring combat strategy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: barren alien planet surface; cave ecosystems; natural hazards

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: humanoid alien with peaceful animation; visual similarity to captors but distinct behavioral tells
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: prison camp interior; later tower escape structures; dragon-like creature as shared escape vehicle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: barren alien planet landscape; unfamiliar architecture; lightning-struck laboratory transition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lester begins unarmed and defensive; evasion behaviors dominate early gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: subterranean prison camp with containment design; armed guards; claustrophobic interior architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: forced stealth, puzzle-solving under threat; escape-focused movement patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: presence of Buddy; shift from isolation to paired character animation; cooperation sequences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: duo-based movement; coordinated puzzle-solving; shared survival goals

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: laser pistol weapon acquisition; visual change to player armament; enemy force fields visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: three-mode weapon system available; tactical positioning against armed enemies; strategic use of environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: traversal from prison complex → cave system → tower structure; ascending geography
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increasing environmental complexity; puzzle difficulty escalation; enemy encounters become more tactical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lester wounded visual state; Buddy assistance; dragon-like escape creature appears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: final combat encounter; assisted movement indicating ally support; flight animation signifying liberation

environmental_storytelling.technique: visual narrative without UI or text
environmental_storytelling.example: Lester's arrival via Ferrari and underground laboratory establish character identity through cinematic opening; no dialogue or HUD explains the situation—world-state conveyed purely through animation and environment design

Subterranean prison camp design with military organization contrasts sharply with natural cave systems and barren planet surface; visual distinction between infrastructure of control and untamed wilderness
Movement from prison complex → cave system → tower structure creates spatial narrative of escape; vertical ascent visually communicates journey from captivity toward freedom
Laser pistol modes (standard fire, force field, charged shot) matched against enemy identical capabilities; visual mirror indicates parity and need for tactical positioning and environmental leverage

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: instant game-over on any projectile or creature contact
environmental_storytelling.meaning: hostile alien world with no margin for error; reinforces vulnerability and danger pervasively

environmental_storytelling.example: Characters speak in unintelligible alien language; lack of translation maintains player disorientation and world-state alienation; meaning conveyed through animation and context only

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: evasion, jumping, kicking small creatures; defensive posture and movement patterns
tactical positioning, three-mode weapon use, strategic force field deployment against enemy fire
weakened animation; reliance on Buddy for support; assisted movement indicating injury state
prisoner animation; escape-focused behavior; coordination with Lester
puzzle cooperation, shared navigation, physical support during final escape; dragon-creature interaction
organized pursuit; laser weapon fire with three-mode capability matching player weapon; force field deployment to block player shots
aggressive postures; varied attack patterns based on creature type; environmental integration (cave dwelling, surface roaming)

notes: Another World communicates world-state and danger primarily through cinematic animation, environmental design, and behavioral visual tells rather than explicit UI elements. The absence of HUD (except oxygen bar during water sequences) and intelligible dialogue forces the player to read danger and faction identity through visual manifestation. The one-hit-death mechanic is a core world-state signal: the alien world is inherently lethal and offers no mercy. Buddy's visual distinction from captors and behavioral animation establish faction alliance despite shared humanoid alien design. The progression from unarmed → armed → weakened creates a narrative arc visible through character animation changes. Architectural contrast between prison infrastructure and natural environments visually reinforces the political situation of oppression versus liberation. The laser pistol's three-mode system (standard, force field, charged) is mirrored in enemies, creating visual parity that communicates need for tactical thinking. Checkpoint system (alphanumeric codes) provides world-state safety markers without breaking cinematic immersion.
